name: Limburgish

sources.author: Gussenhoven, Carlos & Aarts, Flor
sources.title: The dialect of Maastricht
sources.publisher: Journal of the International Phonetic Association 29(2)
sources.notes: Illustration of a Limburgish variety incl. its tone contrast; a model source for a future spec.

notes: Limburgish (Limburgs) — West Germanic language/dialect group of the Meuse-Rhenish area (Netherlands, Belgium, Germany). THE GRAPHEME INVENTORY IS NOT YET SPECIFIED here (graphemes/allophones are empty). Limburgish is written in the Latin script but has no single accepted orthography: Dutch-based, German-based and the 2003 Veldeke spelling all coexist, and the varieties differ substantially in their (very large) vowel inventories. It is furthermore a tonal language, contrasting sleeptoon (dragging tone) and stoottoon (push tone), which the spellings do not mark. A grapheme map would have to commit to one variety plus one spelling norm and cite it.

ancestors.notes: Germanic — the classification node this language sits under.
